summary: Postinstall credential stealer exfiltrating SSH keys and secret env vars to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: The package advertises itself as an 'Unofficial opensea-sdk SDK' but its main entry (index.js) is an empty stub (module.exports={}). The only functional code runs from the postinstall lifecycle hook, which on `npm install` walks the installer's current working directory, home directory, and ~/.ssh for private key material (.pem,.key, id_rsa, id_ed25519,.cred), reads.env files, and enumerates process.env filtering for keys matching PRIVATE, SECRET, TOKEN, API_KEY, PASSWORD, MNEMONIC, SEED, WALLET, and AWS. The collected private keys, secret environment values, hostname, username, and cwd are then POSTed to a hardcoded remote endpoint at http://107.161.90.180:7777 over plain HTTP. The package name impersonates the legitimate OpenSea SDK (opensea-js) namespace, functioning as a lure to trigger the credential harvester on install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
